name: Hendrickson Bus Corp
principals: Kevin Clifford (longtime president, pre-sale); Clifford family (founders, 1955 purchase)
fleet_size_est: ~150 (Hendrickson DOT 382112, 152 power units / 93 drivers); affiliated Huntington Coach/Hendrickson/Dell combined ~900 vehicles
districts_or_customers: Long Island school districts incl. Oyster Bay-East Norwich; combined affiliated group serves ~25 Long Island districts
recent_news: Feb 2023: Hendrickson Bus joined Beacon Mobility (Audax-backed) alongside Huntington Coach Corp, Huntington Coach LLC and Dell Transportation - now under PE platform. NOTE: source record lists East Meredith, NY (684 County Hwy 10), which web records show is a residential property; no upstate operator surfaces by this name - likely a data-attribution error against the Bayville, NY Hendrickson Bus Corp, which has been PE-acquired.
website: https://hendrickson-bus-corp.hub.biz/ (third-party listing); operating company under beaconmobility.com
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. Hendrickson Bus Corp (Bayville, NY) was acquired by Beacon Mobility (Audax PE) in Feb 2023 and is now part of Summit's direct competitor platform. Source record's East Meredith address appears to be a data error (private residence); no separate independent operator confirmed at that address.

name: Baumann & Sons Buses
owner_or_parent: Defunct - filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy 2020 (EDNY 8:20-bk-72121); ceased operations
principals: Baumann family (historical owners); attorney announced shutdown 2020
fleet_size_est: historical ~1,000+ buses across affiliated entities (Baumann & Sons, Baumann Bus Company, Acme Bus, Brookset)
districts_or_customers: Historically served Freeport, Baldwin, Rockville Centre, Valley Stream, Malverne, East Meadow, Merrick, Bellmore, Roslyn, Hewlett-Woodmere, Long Beach school districts on Long Island
recent_news: April 2020: Baumann & Sons Buses and affiliates (Baumann Bus, Acme, Brookset) shut down for good citing COVID; ~1,400 employees laid off. Filed Chapter 11 in U.S. Bankruptcy Court EDNY (case 8:20-bk-72121). Copiague property later sold to Sitex Group. Some online directory listings persist but operations effectively ended.
fit_notes: NOT a viable tuck-in target. Company filed for bankruptcy and ceased operations in April 2020 due to COVID; sold Copiague yard; ~1,400 employees terminated. Any current trade name presence is residual directory listings only. Skip.

name: Gomes School Bus Service
fleet_size_est: ~20-30 (estimate based on 60 employees and small island operation; not publicly disclosed)
service_types: school, charter, driver's education, fleet maintenance/auto repair
districts_or_customers: Hawaii Department of Education contracts on Oahu (windward side - Kaneohe/Kailua area); also operates Gomes Drivers Ed School and Gomes Bus Fleet Maintenance auto-repair shop
recent_news: No material M&A or ownership-change news found. Active small family operator with A+ BBB rating (file since 1990). Diversified into driver's-ed and third-party fleet maintenance (gomesautorepair.com).
website: https://gomesdriversed.com/ (driver's-ed sister business); company has BBB profile and Facebook page; no dedicated bus-service site found
fit_notes: Genuine independent family-owned HI operator with 65+ year history under Gomes family, ancillary driver's-ed and maintenance shop. HI is outside Summit's stated Mainland focus, and Hawaii's school-bus market is highly concentrated under Roberts Hawaii. Tuck-in fit is low strategically unless Summit pursues HI footprint; otherwise pass.

name: Dudoit's Bus Service Inc
principals: Moana Dudoit (CEO/owner, inherited from her father); Raquel Dudoit (per D&B listing)
fleet_size_est: ~8-10 buses (8-bus DOE contract per Civil Beat 2011; likely similar today)
employees_est: ~20-25 (Civil Beat noted 22 employees; record states 48 - possibly seasonal/overstated)
service_types: school (Hawaii DOE Get On Board program), special-education transport on Molokai
districts_or_customers: Hawaii DOE contract carrier for Molokai island routes since 1964; one of three Molokai operators alongside Molokai Transportation Associates and Spencer's Bus Service
recent_news: No recent M&A. Long-tenured Molokai operator; founder's daughter Moana Dudoit profiled in 2013 Maui News obituary/feature on father (started as taxi 1964, evolved to school bus). Continues to win HI DOE annual contracts under Get On Board program.
website: None confirmed (source data 'Moanasflorist.com' appears to be a different Dudoit-family florist business)
fit_notes: Very small island-only operator (Molokai, pop ~7,300). Genuine family-owned, multi-generational. Fleet est. <10 buses makes it sub-scale even as a Hawaii tuck-in. Geographic isolation and tiny revenue base = poor strategic fit for Summit Mainland platform. Skip.

name: Johnson School Bus
owner_or_parent: Landmark Student Transportation (Canadian PE-backed school-bus consolidator)
principals: Pre-sale: Steve Johnson (President), Dan Johnson (GM), Judy Johnson - third-generation owners; founder Aaron W. Johnson (1942). Family retained as advisors post-deal.
fleet_size_est: ~450-500 buses across 10-11 SE Wisconsin terminals (per company site and 2019 news)
employees_est: ~500 PT + 50 FT (company-wide); source record's 52 likely Beaver Dam location only
service_types: school, charter, special-needs/STS (shared-ride taxi for Ozaukee/Washington counties via Specialized Transport Services subsidiary)
districts_or_customers: ~14 SE Wisconsin school districts; locations include West Bend (HQ), Beaver Dam, Campbellsport, Cedar Grove, Fond du Lac, Horicon, Kewaskum, Menomonee Falls, Oakfield, Plymouth, Port Washington
recent_news: Feb 2019: Johnson School Bus Service and STS subsidiary sold to Landmark Student Transportation (Canada-based, backed by Birch Hill Equity Partners / consolidator platform). Johnson family stayed on as advisors; trade names retained. Now part of Landmark group.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in. Sold to Landmark Student Transportation (Canadian PE-backed consolidator) in Feb 2019; Beaver Dam terminal is one of ~11 Landmark-owned Johnson locations. Could only be acquired as part of a Landmark portfolio carve-out or full-platform deal.
